DefinitionType.description: The base abstract type for all schema definitions. It provides metadata common to all types such as descriptions and deprecation status.

description.description: A brief explanation of the purpose and usage of this type.

type.description: The discriminator value used to identify the specific definition subclass.

deprecated.description: Indicates if this type is legacy and should no longer be used in new implementations.

StructDefinitionType.description: Represents a fixed-structure object (class/record). It supports inheritance and explicit property definitions.

parent.description: A reference to another struct from which this struct inherits properties.

base.description: If true, this struct acts as an abstract template and cannot be instantiated directly.

properties.description: A map of property names to their respective types defining the structure of the object.

discriminator.description: The property name used to distinguish between different implementations of a base struct.

mapping.description: Maps discriminator values to their concrete definition names for polymorphic handling.

CollectionDefinitionType.description: Abstract base for definitions that hold multiple values of a single type, such as arrays or maps.

schema.description: The type of the elements or values contained within the collection.

MapDefinitionType.description: An object with a dynamic set of keys where every value conforms to the same schema.

ArrayDefinitionType.description: An ordered list of values where every item conforms to the same schema.

PropertyType.description: The base abstract type for all property definitions within a struct or collection.

type.description: The discriminator value used to identify the specific property subclass.

nullable.description: Indicates whether the value can be null.

ScalarPropertyType.description: Abstract base for simple value types like strings, numbers, and booleans.

StringPropertyType.description: Represents a sequence of characters, optionally following a specific format.

format.description: Provides specialized context for the string (e.g., 'date-time').

default.description: The value to be used if the property is not explicitly provided.

NumberPropertyType.description: Represents a floating-point or decimal number.

BooleanPropertyType.description: Represents a true or false value.

CollectionPropertyType.description: Abstract base for properties that reference inline maps or arrays.

schema.description: The schema definition for the items contained in this property's collection.

MapPropertyType.description: A property containing a map of dynamic keys to a consistent value type.

ArrayPropertyType.description: A property containing a list of items of a consistent type.

AnyPropertyType.description: A wildcard property that accepts any valid JSON value (object, array, string, etc.).

GenericPropertyType.description: A placeholder for a type that will be specified at runtime or through template arguments.

name.description: The identifier for the generic (e.g., 'T'). This is matched against template keys in a Reference.

ReferencePropertyType.description: A reference to a defined type in the global 'definitions' map.

target.description: The name of the definition this property points to.

template.description: Maps generic names in the target type to concrete definition names.

TypeSchema.description: The root object of a TypeSchema document containing imports, definitions, and the entry point.

import.description: External TypeSchema documents to include, keyed by an alias namespace.

definitions.description: A dictionary of all types available within this schema document.

root.description: The main entry-point definition for the schema.
